errors.message: Streamable HTTP error: Error POSTing to endpoint: {"error":"invalid_token","error_description":"Missing or invalid access token"}

labels.name: ably_get_presence
labels.description: List the members currently present on a channel (who is online now). REST: GET /channels/{channel}/presence.

labels.name: ably_get_presence_history
labels.description: Retrieve historical presence events (enter/leave/update) for a channel. REST: GET /channels/{channel}/presence/history.

labels.name: ably_get_channel_details
labels.description: Get status and occupancy metrics for a single channel (connections, publishers, subscribers, presence members). REST: GET /channels/{channel}.

labels.name: ably_list_channels
labels.description: Enumerate channels that are currently active in the app. Requires the API key to have the 'channel-metadata' capability. REST: GET /channels.

labels.name: ably_get_stats
labels.description: Retrieve message/connection/channel usage statistics for the app tied to the API key, bucketed by time. REST: GET /stats.

labels.name: ably_get_service_time
labels.description: Return the current Ably service time in ms since epoch. Useful for aligning start/end windows to the server clock. REST: GET /time.

labels.name: ably_publish_message
labels.description: WRITE: publish a message to a channel. Every connected subscriber receives it in real time. This is a real, outward-facing side effect — confirm the channel and payload before calling. REST: POST /channels/{channel}/messages.

labels.name: ably_whoami
labels.description: Return details about the configured control token: the account and user it belongs to and its capabilities. Good first call to confirm control-plane access. Control API: GET /me.

labels.name: ably_list_apps
labels.description: List all apps in the account (id, name, status, tls-only). Account id is resolved automatically from the control token when ABLY_ACCOUNT_ID is unset. Control API: GET /accounts/{account_id}/apps.

labels.name: ably_list_keys
labels.description: List the API keys for an app, including their capabilities (but not the full secret). Control API: GET /apps/{app_id}/keys.

labels.name: ably_list_namespaces
labels.description: List the channel namespaces (channel-rule prefixes) configured for an app, e.g. persistence and push settings. Control API: GET /apps/{app_id}/namespaces.

labels.name: ably_list_queues
labels.description: List the Ably Queues (AMQP/STOMP message queues) provisioned for an app, with their state and messaging stats. Control API: GET /apps/{app_id}/queues.

labels.name: ably_list_rules
labels.description: List the integration rules (reactor rules: webhooks, AWS Lambda/SQS/Kinesis, Kafka, Pulsar, etc.) configured for an app. Control API: GET /apps/{app_id}/rules.

labels.name: ably_get_account_stats
labels.description: Retrieve aggregated usage statistics across the whole account, bucketed by time. Account id is resolved from the control token when ABLY_ACCOUNT_ID is unset. Control API: GET /accounts/{account_id}/stats.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "ably_publish_message" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: WRITE: publish a message to a channel. Every connected subscriber receives it in real time. This is a real, outward-facing side effect — confirm the channel and…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "ably_list_keys" references sensitive artifacts (secret). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: List the API keys for an app, including their capabilities (but not the full secret). Control API: GET /apps/{app_id}/keys.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: ably_get_channel_history, ably_get_stats, ably_list_queues · Private data: ably_list_channels, ably_get_stats, ably_whoami, ably_list_apps, ably_list_keys, ably_get_account_stats · Public sink: ably_publish_message.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: ably_get_channel_history, ably_get_stats, ably_list_queues · Private data: ably_list_channels, ably_get_stats, ably_whoami, ably_list_apps, ably_list_keys, ably_get_account_stats · Public sink: ably_publish_message

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
